ID:       ddx-424fdac4
Title:    Ratify DDx specs for Fizeau-owned routing and no-agent cleanup
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:docs, area:spec, area:fizeau, kind:design, breaking, decomposed
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-f088a3fd
Deps:     ddx-e2586179, ddx-242d2781, ddx-55559451, ddx-119ddb10, ddx-b42d73be
Desc:     PROBLEM
Implementation agents need durable specs before removing `ddx agent`, renaming DDx internals, or consuming Fizeau contract changes. Today the specs still mix the old `agent` vocabulary with the desired DDx/Fizeau boundary, so agents can reasonably follow stale instructions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
FEAT-001 still describes an `Agent Passthrough` structural mount for `ddx agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-001-cli.md:70-84. FEAT-006 still describes the upstream invocation boundary as `agentlib.DdxAgent` and `DDx Agent Service` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:1-23. FEAT-010 still says layer 1 consumes upstream `ddx-agent` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:16-19. TP-020 remains a historical DDx-side routing test plan even though Fizeau owns routing. docs/migrations/routing-config.md:1-13 removes some old routing keys but does not yet document the full `agent.*` removal / Fizeau-vs-execution config split.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the specs before implementation. Required docs: FEAT-001, FEAT-006 or its renamed replacement, FEAT-010, ADR-024, TP-020, docs/migrations/routing-config.md, docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md, and DDx skill references. The specs must state: DDx owns task execution (`run`, `try`, `work`); Fizeau owns routing, provider/model discovery, native transcript/session rendering, and fuzzy model constraint resolution; `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are explicit operator passthrough only; DDx passes raw strings unchanged; `ddx agent` is removed without deprecation; and `agent` is allowed only for actual external AI agent concepts.

NON-SCOPE
No production code removal in this bead. No Fizeau implementation work. No compatibility alias design. No generated CLI docs rebuild unless the docs generator is part of the spec-doc workflow.

UPSTREAM REFERENCES
- fizeau-61668fd3 for native harness `agent` -> `fiz` contract identity
- fizeau-400b57fe for Fizeau-owned model alias/fuzzy matching

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Dependencies: none; this bead comes first.
Accept:   1. FEAT-001 removes the `ddx agent` structural mount and defines only `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, and any Fizeau diagnostics namespace that remains, with no aliases.
2. FEAT-006 is renamed or rewritten as the DDx consumer spec for Fizeau, and no longer presents `agentlib.DdxAgent` / `ddx-agent` as the desired contract name.
3. FEAT-010 names DDx-owned packages/roles using task execution vocabulary (`run`, `try`, `work`, `taskexec`, `fizeauadapter`) rather than `internal/agent`.
4. ADR-024 says escalation changes only MinPower/MaxPower/request facts and Fizeau chooses the concrete route.
5. TP-020 is replaced or superseded with a DDx boundary test plan proving DDx does not normalize, fuzzy-match, or route models.
6. docs/migrations/routing-config.md documents removal of `agent.*` defaults and the split between Fizeau routing config and DDx execution config.
7. DDx skill references explain explicit passthrough and Fizeau-owned model matching using examples such as `--model qwen36`, while stating DDx passes the raw string unchanged.
8. `rg -n "ddx agent|ddx-agent|agentlib.DdxAgent|Agent Service|cli/internal/agent" docs .agents/skills .claude/skills skills` returns only audited historical or literal-external-agent hits documented in the change.
9. `ddx doc validate` passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are listed in the bead notes.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

ID:       ddx-ddacd4ff
Title:    C14: delete ExecuteBead* shims and aliases; verify all callers ported
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, refactor, kind:cleanup
Parent:   ddx-5cb6e6cd
Deps:     ddx-387a0178, ddx-3ee4ddcf
Desc:     PROBLEM
After the C13 refactor (ddx-387a0178), shim typedefs of the form ExecuteBeadWorker, ExecuteBeadReport, ExecuteBeadResult, ExecuteBeadStatus, ExecuteBeadOutcome remain in the codebase as temporary aliases. These shims prevent callers from adopting the canonical names and leave dead identifier surface that grep/tooling cannot distinguish from active usage.

ROOT CAUSE
C13 (ddx-387a0178) introduces the canonical type names as part of the file/identifier rename step and adds shim typedefs for backward compat during the migration window. The shim typedefs live in the files renamed by C13 (exact file:line determined after C13 lands). The CLI aliases ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead continue to register live cobra commands after C13, pointing at logic now accessible via ddx work and ddx try.

PROPOSED FIX
- Sweep all callsites off the shim names (grep for ExecuteBeadWorker | ExecuteBeadReport | ExecuteBeadResult | ExecuteBead\b | ExecuteBeadStatus | ExecuteBeadOutcome in cli/).
- Delete the shim typedefs from C13.
- Remove cobra command registrations for ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead; replace with a cobra RunE that prints a deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
- Verify: grep returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test files.

NON-SCOPE
- Deleting the entire ddx agent command group (that is ddx-d8474e0e, a separate bead).
- Changing execute-loop/try behavior.
Accept:   1. grep -rn 'ExecuteBeadWorker\|ExecuteBeadReport\|ExecuteBeadResult\|ExecuteBead\b\|ExecuteBeadStatus\|ExecuteBeadOutcome' cli/ returns 0 hits in non-deleted, non-test source files.
2. ddx agent execute-loop and ddx agent execute-bead cobra commands replaced with deprecation message pointing at ddx work / ddx try.
3. TestNoBuildRegressions_AfterShimDelete: cd cli && go build ./... green.
4. cd cli && go test ./... green.
5. Server worker integration test green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
7. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.RecoverOrphans (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:383) is invoked from the ddx try / ddx work code path. Today it is called ONLY from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:168 across the entire CLI tree (verified by grep). If it is not yet wired into the try/work path, lift the call into ExecuteBeadWithConfig (engine) or into try.Attempt before this bead's cobra-replacement step. A regression test (TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.
8. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.BuildLandingGateContext (cli/internal/agent/landing_gate_context.go:28) is invoked when landing through the ddx try CLI path. Today it is called from cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:206 and from cli/internal/server/workers.go:1700 (server-side worker), but NOT from the CLI try/work path (verified by grep). If not wired through, lift into the landing helper used by try.Attempt. A regression test (TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, in cli/cmd/try_test.go) must assert the behavior survives.
9. BEFORE replacing the cobra command, verify agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService preflight (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:390, called at cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:160-164 when no AgentRunnerOverride) is either invoked from ddx try / ddx work (CLI) or explicitly documented as N/A in this bead's notes with rationale. Today try.go does not call it.
10. INTERSECTIONS: ddx-d8474e0e (full ddx agent retirement) reuses the same engine wiring; the lifts in AC #7-9 are prerequisites for both this bead and ddx-d8474e0e. ddx-a1ae37ff (commit-scoping --no-verify fix) is independent and lands first.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the scope of retiring the `ddx agent` CLI surface. The repo is at /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d8474e0e-20260510T013243-b86bf611/

Please investigate and report:

1. Contents of cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (full file - how many cobra commands registered, what subcommands exist)

2. Contents of cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (lines 155-220 specifically, where RecoverOrphans, BuildLandingGateContext, and ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService are called)

3. Contents of cli/cmd/try.go and cli/cmd/try_test.go - what's there, what tests exist (especially: TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn, TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding, TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead, TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2)

4. In cli/internal/agent/: check execute_bead_orchestrator.go lines around 383 (RecoverOrphans), landing_gate_context.go lines around 28 (BuildLandingGateContext), service_run.go lines around 390 (ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService) - what do these functions do?

5. How many lines are in: agent_execute_bead_test.go, agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go, agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go, execute_bead_e2e_routing_test.go, agent_execute_loop_test.go?

6. Does cli/cmd/work_test.go have TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop?

7. What does cli/cmd/work.go look like?

Report findings concisely - focus on what I need to know to determine if this bead can be executed or is blocked by missing prerequisites.

47	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
48	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
49	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
50	
51	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
52	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
53	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit

93		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
94		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
98	
99		return cmd
100	}
101	
102	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
103	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
104		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
105			return err
106		}
107	
108		beadID := args[0]
109	
110		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
111		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
112	
113		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
114		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before
115		// any bead state changes.
116		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
117		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
118			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
119		})
120	
121		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
122		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
123		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
124		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
125		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
126		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
127		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
128		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
129		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")
130		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
131		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
132		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
133		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
134		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
135	
136		if noReview && !noReviewAck {
137			return fmt.Errorf("--no-review requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing (break-glass acknowledgement)")
138		}
139	
140		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
141	
142		// Pre-flight: look up the bead.
143		target, err := store.Get(beadID)
144		if err != nil {
145			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead not found: %s\n", beadID)
146			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
147		}
148	
149		// Pre-flight: verify the bead is in a claimable state.
150		// Claimable = open, or stale in_progress (the loop handles stale reclaim).
151		switch target.Status {
152		case bead.StatusOpen, bead.StatusInProgress:
153			// allowed — loop's Claim() handles stale-heartbeat reclaim
154		default:
155			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not claimable (status=%s)\n", target.Status)
156			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
157		}
158	
159		// Pre-flight: check for unmet dependencies.
160		if unmet := unmetDeps(store, target); len(unmet) > 0 {
161			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead has unmet dependencies: %s\n", strings.Join(unmet, ", "))
162			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
163		}
164	
165		// Wrap the real store so that ReadyExecution() returns only the target bead.
166		// All other operations pass through to the underlying store unchanged.
167		forcedStore := &singleBeadStore{Store: store, target: *target}
168	
169		// Set up progress logging (same as runAgentExecuteLoopImpl).
170		loopSessionID := fmt.Sprintf("agent-loop-%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
171		loopLogDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, agent.DefaultLogDir)
172		_ = os.MkdirAll(loopLogDir, 0o755)
173		loopLogPath := filepath.Join(loopLogDir, loopSessionID+".jsonl")
174		var loopSink io.Writer
175		if lf, openErr := os.OpenFile(loopLogPath, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644); openErr == nil {
176			loopSink = lf
177			defer lf.Close() //nolint:errcheck
178		}
179	
180		// Worker-probe (best-effort server mirror; non-blocking).
181		probeIdent := workerprobe.Identity{
182			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
183			Harness:      harness,
184			Model:        model,
185			ExecutorPID:  os.Getpid(),
186			ExecutorHost: hostnameOrEmpty(),
187			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
188		}
189		probe := workerprobe.New(probeIdent, workerprobe.Config{
190			AddrFunc: serverpkg.ReadServerAddr,
191		})
192		probeCtx, probeCancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
193		probe.Start(probeCtx)
194		defer func() {
195			probeCancel()
196			probe.Stop()
197		}()
198		if loopSink != nil {
199			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(loopSink, probe)
200		} else {
201			loopSink = workerprobe.TeeJSONL(io.Discard, probe)
202		}
203	
204		// Process-local LandCoordinator.
205		localCoord := serverpkg.NewLocalLandCoordinator(projectRoot, agent.RealLandingGitOps{})
206		defer localCoord.Stop()
207	
208		// Post-merge reviewer (on by default, skipped via --no-review).
209		var reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
210		if !noReview {
211			reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
212				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
213				BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
214				BeadEvents:  bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
215				Harness:     reviewHarness,
216				Model:       reviewModel,
217			}
218		}
219	
220		profile = agent.NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile)
221	
222		// Determine whether zero-config auto-route applies (same logic as runWork).
223		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
224			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
225		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
226		resourceChecker := buildCLIResourceChecker(projectRoot, f.resourceCheckerOverride)
227	
228		// Build the executor: either the test override or the real single-tier executor.
229		var executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
230		if f.tryExecutorOverride != nil {
231			executor = f.tryExecutorOverride
232		} else {
233			executor = agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, execBeadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
234				var inferredTier escalation.ModelTier
235				if autoInferTier {
236					if b, getErr := store.Get(execBeadID); getErr == nil {
237						inferredTier = escalation.InferTier(b)
238					} else {
239						inferredTier = escalation.TierCheap
240					}
241				}
242	
243				loopOverrides := config.CLIOverrides{
244					Harness:           harness,
245					Model:             model,
246					Provider:          provider,
247					ModelRef:          modelRef,
248					Profile:           profile,
249					Effort:            effort,
250					MinPower:          minPower,
251					MaxPower:          maxPower,
252					OpaquePassthrough: true, // ddx try treats flags as opaque passthrough
253				}
254				if requestTimeout > 0 {
255					loopOverrides.ProviderRequestTimeout = &requestTimeout
256				}
257				attemptRcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, loopOverrides)
258				gitOps := &agent.RealGitOps{}
259				res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, projectRoot, execBeadID, attemptRcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
260					FromRev:         fromRev,
261					Output:          cmd.OutOrStdout(),
262					BeadEvents:      bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
263					ResourceChecker: resourceChecker,
264				}, gitOps)
265				if execErr != nil && res == nil {
266					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}, execErr
267				}
268				if res != nil && agent.IsResourceExhaustedStatus(res.Status) {
269					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
270				}
271				if execErr != nil {
272					agent.MarkResultExecutionError(res, execErr)
273					return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
274				}
275				if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
276					targetBead, _ := store.Get(execBeadID)
277					landRes, _, landErr := agent.SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
278						ctx, projectRoot, targetBead, res,
279						func(req agent.LandRequest) (*agent.LandResult, error) { return localCoord.Submit(req) },
280						bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
281						resolveClaimAssignee(), "ddx try",
282						nil,
283					)
284					if landErr == nil {
285						agent.ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res, landRes)
286						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
287					} else {
288						agent.MarkResultLandError(projectRoot, res, landErr)
289						_ = agent.WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact(projectRoot, res)
290						return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, string(inferredTier)), nil
291					}
292				} else if res != nil && (res.Outcome == agent.ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed || res.ExitCode != 0) {
293					if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
294						res.Outcome = "preserved"
295					} else {
296						res.Outcome = "error"
297					}
298					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
299				} else if res != nil && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
300					res.Outcome = "no-changes"
301					res.Status = agent.ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
302				}
303				tierStr := ""
304				if inferredTier != "" {
305					tierStr = string(inferredTier)
306				}
307				return agent.ReportFromExecuteBeadResult(res, tierStr), nil
308			})
309		}
310	
311		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
312			Store:    forcedStore,
313			Executor: executor,
314			Reviewer: reviewer,
315		}
316	
317		overrides := config.CLIOverrides{
318			Assignee: resolveClaimAssignee(),
319			Harness:  harness,
320			Model:    model,
321			Provider: provider,
322			ModelRef: modelRef,
323			Profile:  profile,
324			Effort:   effort,
325		}
326		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, overrides)
327		if err != nil {
328			return fmt.Errorf("load resolved config: %w", err)
329		}
330		if _, err := resourceChecker.Check(cmd.Context()); err != nil {
331			if resErr, ok := err.(*agent.ResourceExhaustedError); ok && resErr != nil {
332				fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), resErr.Error())
333			}
334			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: err.Error()}
335		}
336	
337		cliLandingOps := agent.RealLandingGitOps{}
338		var qualityRunner agent.AgentRunner
339		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
340			qualityRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
341		}
342		lintHook := agent.NewPreDispatchLintHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
343		triageHook := agent.NewPostAttemptTriageHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner, nil)
344		result, runErr := worker.Run(cmd.Context(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
345			Mode:                  executeloop.ModeOnce,
346			Log:                   cmd.OutOrStdout(),
347			EventSink:             loopSink,
348			WorkerID:              resolveClaimAssignee(),
349			ProjectRoot:           projectRoot,
350			ResourceChecker:       resourceChecker,
351			SessionID:             loopSessionID,
352			PreClaimHook:          buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
353			PreDispatchLintHook:   lintHook,
354			PostAttemptTriageHook: triageHook,
355			NoReview:              noReview,
356		})
357		if runErr != nil {
358			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
359				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
360			}
361			return runErr
362		}
363	
364		// Report outcome.
365		if result.NoReadyWork {
366			// Bead was not in the execution-ready queue even after pre-flight passed.
367			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
368			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
369			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
370			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
371		}
372	
373		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
374			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
375		}
376		report := result.Results[0]
377		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
378		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.RequestedTier != "" {
379			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: tier=%s source=%s\n", intent.RequestedTier, intent.Source)
380		}
381	
382		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
383	}
384	
385	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
386		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
387		if report.Detail != "" {
388			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
389		}
390		if report.ResultRev != "" {
391			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
392		}
393		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
394			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
395		}
396	}
397	
398	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
399	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
400		switch status {
401		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
402			return nil // exit 0
403		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
404			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
405			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
406		default:
407			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
408			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
409		}
410	}
411	
412	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
413	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
414	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
415		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
416		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
417			return nil
418		}
419		var unmet []string
420		for _, depID := range depIDs {
421			dep, err := store.Get(depID)
422			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
423				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
424			}
425		}
426		return unmet
427	}
428	
429	// singleBeadStore wraps a *bead.Store and overrides ReadyExecution to return
430	// only a single target bead. All other operations are delegated unchanged.
431	// This allows the execute-bead worker to treat the forced bead as if it were
432	// the only entry in the execution-ready queue.
433	type singleBeadStore struct {
434		*bead.Store
435		target bead.Bead
436	}
437	
438	func (s *singleBeadStore) ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error) {
439		return []bead.Bead{s.target}, nil
440	}

19	type tryHookRunnerStub struct {
20		t            *testing.T
21		promptSource []string
22	}
23	
24	type blockingTryExecutor struct {
25		started chan struct{}
26		once    sync.Once
27	}
28	
29	func (e *blockingTryExecutor) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
30		e.once.Do(func() { close(e.started) })
31		<-ctx.Done()
32		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
33			BeadID:    beadID,
34			Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
35			Detail:    ctx.Err().Error(),
36			BaseRev:   "feedface",
37			ResultRev: "feedface",
38		}, ctx.Err()
39	}

41	func runInterruptedTryCommand(t *testing.T) (*bead.Store, string, error) {
42		t.Helper()
43	
44		t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
45		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
46		env.CreateDefaultConfig()
47		store := bead.NewStore(env.Dir + "/.ddx")
48		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
49		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
50			ID:    "try-interrupt-001",
51			Title: "Interrupted try bead",
52		}))
53	
54		factory := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir)
55		runner := &blockingTryExecutor{started: make(chan struct{})}
56		factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}
57		factory.tryExecutorOverride = runner
58		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
59	
60		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
61		root.SetContext(ctx)
62	
63		var buf bytes.Buffer
64		root.SetOut(&buf)
65		root.SetErr(&buf)
66		root.SetArgs([]string{"try", "try-interrupt-001", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing"})
67	
68		done := make(chan error, 1)
69		go func() {
70			done <- root.Execute()
71		}()
72	
73		select {
74		case <-runner.started:
75		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
76			t.Fatal("try executor did not start")
77		}
78		cancel()
79	
80		var err error
81		select {
82		case err = <-done:
83		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
84			t.Fatal("try command did not return after cancel")
85		}
86	
87		return store, buf.String(), err
88	}
89	
90	func (r *tryHookRunnerStub) Run(opts agent.RunArgs) (*agent.Result, error) {
91		r.promptSource = append(r.promptSource, opts.PromptSource)
92		switch opts.PromptSource {
93		case "bead-lifecycle-intake":
94			return &agent.Result{
95				ExitCode: 0,
96				Output:   `{"classification":"atomic","confidence":0.99,"reasoning":"single-slice"}`,
97			}, nil
98		case "bead-lifecycle-lint":
99			return &agent.Result{
100				ExitCode: 0,
101				Output:   `{"score":9,"rationale":"ok","suggested_fixes":[],"waivers_applied":[]}`,
102			}, nil
103		case "bead-lifecycle-triage":
104			return &agent.Result{
105				ExitCode: 0,
106				Output:   `{"classification":"transport","recommended_action":"release_claim_retry","rationale":"transient","suggested_amendments":[],"suggested_followup_beads":[]}`,
107			}, nil
108		default:
109			r.t.Fatalf("unexpected prompt source: %q", opts.PromptSource)
110			return nil, nil
111		}
112	}

11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough
25	constraints forwarded to Fizeau unchanged. DDx does not validate these values
26	or branch on them; Fizeau owns routing within the requested power
27	bounds.
28	
29	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
30	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
31	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
32	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
33	
34	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
35	  drained     — no ready beads remain
36	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
37	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
38	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
39	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
40	
41	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
42	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
43	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
44	`,
45			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue and exit
46	  ddx work
47	
48	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
49	  ddx work --once
50	
51	  # Watch for newly-ready work after the current queue drains
52	  ddx work --watch
53	
54	  # Watch with a shorter idle scan interval
55	  ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s
56	
57	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
58	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
59	
60	  # Skip review only with the break-glass acknowledgement flag
61	  ddx work --once --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing
62	
63	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
64	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
65			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
66			RunE: f.runWork,
67		}
68	
69		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
70		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
71		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
72		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
73		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
74		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
76		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
77		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model reference passthrough (e.g. code-medium); resolved by Fizeau")
78		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
79		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
80		cmd.Flags().Bool("watch", false, "Keep watching for newly-ready beads after the current queue drains")
81		cmd.Flags().Duration("idle-interval", 30*time.Second, "Sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode")
82		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
83		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
84		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
85		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
86		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
87		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
88		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
89		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
90		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
91		// Per-bead rate-limit retry budget (ddx-c6e3db02 / TD-031 §8.4).
92		cmd.Flags().Duration("rate-limit-max-wait", agent.RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget,
93			"Per-bead total wait budget for HTTP 429 / rate-limit retries (default 5m). 0 keeps the default; negative disables retry.")
94		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
95		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
96		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
97		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
98	
99		// Register "ddx work plan" and "ddx work focus" as subcommands of "ddx work".
100		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkPlanCommand())
101		cmd.AddCommand(f.newWorkFocusCommand())
102	
103		return cmd
104	}
105	
106	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
107	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
108	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
109	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
110	// by this layer.
111	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
113			return err
114		}
115	
116		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
120		// Suppressed in JSON mode to avoid corrupting machine-readable output
121		// (stderr and stdout are merged by some test helpers and log aggregators).
122		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
123		if !asJSON {
124			preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
125			f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
126				emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
127			})
128		}
129	
130		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true, "")
131	}

10	// BuildLandingGateContext prepares the inputs the gate evaluator needs to
11	// score a worker's result before LandBeadResult decides merge-vs-preserve:
12	//
13	//  1. Pin res.ResultRev to refs/ddx/gate-pins/<beadID>/<attemptID>
14	//     so the SHA stays alive while the ephemeral worktree exists.
15	//  2. WorktreeAdd a temp worktree (under os.TempDir() with prefix
16	//     "ddx-gate-wt-") at the pinned ref so gate evaluators can read the
17	//     result tree without disturbing the main worktree.
18	//  3. Read the worker manifest from that result tree and extract governing IDs.
19	//     When the manifest declares no governing IDs the temporary worktree is
20	//     removed immediately and the caller skips gate evaluation.
21	//
22	// The returned cleanup closure removes the worktree and deletes the pin ref.
23	// Callers MUST defer cleanup() before returning to avoid orphaned worktrees.
24	//
25	// On any error the function attempts to roll back partial state (pin+remove)
26	// and returns ("", nil, noop, err).
27	func BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps GitOps) (wtPath string, governIDs []string, cleanup func(), err error) {
28		noop := func() {}
29	
30		if res == nil || res.ManifestFile == "" {
31			return "", nil, noop, nil
32		}
33		if res.ResultRev == "" {
34			return "", nil, noop, fmt.Errorf("BuildLandingGateContext: ResultRev empty, cannot pin")
35		}
36	
37		// Pin ResultRev so the worktree-add target stays alive even though the worker
38		// worktree was removed. Use bead+attempt to avoid collisions across attempts.

/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d8474e0e-20260510T013243-b86bf611/.agents/skills/.ddx/executions/20260414T053633-822f2771/prompt.md:The current execute-bead worker does WAY too much: creates a worktree, runs the agent, evaluates required gates, decides merge-by-default vs preserve vs land-conflict, writes preserve refs, synthesizes commits on IsDirty, classifies 7+ outcomes, manages heartbeats, handles claim/unclaim contention, runs recoverOrphans on stale worktrees. This surface is why (a) two concurrent workers race on the main-branch merge and the loser's good work ends up under refs/ddx/iterations/ needing manual rescue, (b) no_changes gets misclassified as synthesized-commit success, (c) the gate-enforcement path and the merge path are entangled, and (d) the worker needs special privileges (update-ref, merge, rebase) that a simple task-runner should never have.

The correct design is the one the operator just described: execute-bead is a thin worker. 'I have been handed a worktree and a task. I will execute that task and make commits in the worktree. I will stream progress events to ddx server. I will notify ddx server when I am done, reporting the worktree branch and the commit(s) I made. Then I exit.' That's it.

Everything else — merge to main, conflict resolution, required-gate evaluation, preserve-under-ref, decide whether to close the bead — moves to the PARENT process (the operator, or a driver tool running interactively in the terminal). Merge conflicts are resolved interactively the same as any other merge. A human (or an outer agent with full git authority) reviews the agent's commits and decides what to do with them.

This simplification deletes most of ExecuteBead() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and moves:
- resolveBase / baseRev tracking → parent
- Merge() call → parent
- preserveRef / UpdateRef / refs/ddx/iterations/ writes → parent
- evaluateRequiredGates → parent (or a separate 'ddx agent check' step)
- outcome classification 'merged/preserved/land-conflict/error' → parent
- recoverOrphans → parent (cleanup runs when parent next launches workers)
- heartbeatIsStale reclaim logic → parent (parent tracks worker health)

What the worker keeps:
- Take a worktree dir + task description (prompt) as input
- Launch the agent harness with the prompt
- Stream progress events to ddx server (session log + structured JSONL)
- On exit, emit a final event: worktree path, branch, commit shas (or empty), exit code, duration
- That's it

Execute-loop likewise shrinks or goes away. In the clean design, a driver loop in the parent process picks ready beads, creates a /tmp worktree per bead, calls 'ddx agent execute-bead' (worker-only mode), waits for the worker's exit event from ddx server, reviews the result, and merges or discards. When two beads are in flight concurrently, the parent serializes the merge step — no race possible.

```
  ├─ newAgentExecuteBeadCommand()      // agent execute-bead (in agent_execute_bead.go)
  ├─ newAgentExecutionsCommand()       // agent executions (in agent_executions.go)
  ├─ newAgentWorkersCommand()          // agent workers (in agent_workers.go)
  ├─ newAgentProvidersCommand()        // agent providers (in agent_cmd.go)
  ├─ newAgentModelsCommand()           // agent models (in agent_cmd.go)
  ├─ newAgentCheckCommand()            // agent check (in agent_cmd.go)
  ├─ newAgentRouteStatusCommand()      // agent route-status (in agent_cmd.go)
  └─ newAgentMetricsCommand()          // agent metrics (in agent_cmd.go)
```

---

### 2. **agent_execute_bead.go - Lines 155-220 Analysis**

**File size:** 262 lines total

**Critical functions called at lines 155-220:**

1. **Line 161:** `agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService()`
   - Purpose: Pre-flight check for valid harness (skipped if test override present)
   - Error behavior: Returns early if orphan model or unknown harness

2. **Line 168:** `agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)`
   - Purpose: Clean orphaned worktrees before spawning new worker
   - Parent responsibility: Called before worker creation

3. **Line 206:** `agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)`
   - Purpose: Set up gate evaluation context (pins ResultRev, creates ephemeral worktree)
   - Returns: (wtPath, governIDs, cleanup func, error)
   - Soft-fail: Logs warning and skips gate eval on error

4. **Line 191:** `agent.Land()` - Actual landing operation (merge/preserve decision)

---

### 3. **try.go and try_test.go Overview**

**try.go (440 lines):**
- Top-level command: `ddx try <bead-id>`
- Executes a single bead through full production machinery: claim → execute → land → review
- Exit codes: 0 (success/already_satisfied), 1 (preserved/no_changes), 2 (failed/other)
- NOT calling `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` (opaque passthrough of flags)
- Uses `ExecuteBeadWithConfig()` directly, not orchestrator pattern

**try_test.go (473 lines):**
Tests present:
- TestTry_BeadNotFound
- TestTry_BeadClosed
- TestTry_BeadCancelledNotClaimable
- TestTry_BeadUnmetDeps
- TestTry_HappyPath_ClaimsAndExecutes
- TestTryRecordsExecutionRoutingIntent
- TestTryInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsTarget
- TestTryInterrupt_DoesNotReportOperatorCancel
- TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough
- TestTry_HooksWired
- TestTry_NoReviewRequiresAckFlag
- TestTry_CommandRegistered

**Missing tests:** None of the four specifically mentioned tests exist in try_test.go:
- ~~TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn~~ (NOT FOUND)
- ~~TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding~~ (NOT FOUND)
- ~~TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead~~ (NOT FOUND)
- ~~TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2~~ (NOT FOUND)

---

### 4. **Internal Agent Functions (cli/internal/agent/)**

**RecoverOrphans** (execute_bead_orchestrator.go:385)
- Finds worktrees matching `ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"` by basename
- Removes orphaned worktrees regardless of parent directory (legacy .ddx/ vs new $TMPDIR)
- Prunes stale run-state per-attempt; cleans up crashed worker artifacts
- No-op on error (silently returns)

**BuildLandingGateContext** (landing_gate_context.go:27)
- Pins res.ResultRev to refs/ddx/gate-pins/<beadID>/<attemptID>
- Creates ephemeral temp worktree (prefix "ddx-gate-wt-") at pinned ref
- Reads worker manifest and extracts governing IDs
- Returns cleanup closure that removes worktree and deletes pin ref
- Soft-fail on manifest not found: returns ("", nil, noop, nil)

**ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService** (service_run.go:457)
- Checks harness exists and is available
- For model + no provider + no model-ref on "agent" harness: calls ResolveRoute to validate model is routable
- Returns error for unknown harness or unroutable model
- No-op if harness is empty string

---

### 5. **Test File Sizes**

| File | Lines |
|------|-------|
| agent_execute_bead_test.go | 3,024 |
| agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go | 74 |
| agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go | 252 |
| execute_bead_e2e_routing_test.go | 149 |
| agent_execute_loop_test.go | 75 |
| **Total** | **3,574** |

---

### 6. **work.go and work_test.go**

**work.go (131 lines):**
- Top-level `ddx work` command: drains execution-ready queue
- FEAT-010 layer-3 queue drain
- **Does NOT call** `ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService()` (treats flags as opaque passthrough)
- Forwards harness/model as unchecked constraints to agent/routing layer
- Retry-power policy owned by work layer (min-power/max-power)
- Calls `f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl()` for actual work loop

**work_test.go:**
- **TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop:** NOT FOUND
- No matching test for work's JSON schema contract

---

### 7. **Key Retirement Dependencies & Blockers**

**CRITICAL FINDING:** The `ddx agent execute-bead` command has HIGH coupling to the queue-driven work loop:

| Component | Dependency Chain |
|-----------|------------------|
| RecoverOrphans | Called BEFORE worker spawning in agent_execute_bead.go:168 |
| BuildLandingGateContext | Required-gate evaluation path in agent_execute_bead.go:206 |
| ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService | Pre-flight harness/model validation in agent_execute_bead.go:161 |
| ExecuteBeadWithConfig | Core worker invocation (262-line agent_execute_bead.go) |
| LandBeadResult | Orchestration: merge-vs-preserve logic (agent_execute_bead.go:219) |

**Command hierarchy implies retirement order:**
```
ddx agent execute-bead          [PRIMITIVE]
  ↑ used by
ddx agent execute-loop          [QUEUE SUPERVISOR - likely in separate file]
  ↑ used by
ddx work / ddx try              [PRODUCTION SURFACE]
```

---

### 8. **Missing Test Coverage**

The specified high-value tests do NOT exist, which suggests:
- Orphan-recovery behavior is not explicitly validated in try_test.go
- Gate-context application is not isolated in try_test.go
- JSON schema equivalence between try and execute-bead is not verified
- Exit code contract (0/1/2) is tested but not systematized in a single test

---

### Summary: Can This Bead Execute?

**Status: BLOCKED** - Prerequisites are present but critical concerns:

1. ✅ **Scope is clear:** 18 agent subcommands, with `execute-bead` being the primary retirement target
2. ✅ **Infrastructure exists:** RecoverOrphans, BuildLandingGateContext, ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService all defined and callable
3. ✅ **Tests exist:** 3,574 lines of test coverage across execute-bead paths
4. ⚠️ **Test gaps:** Four high-value tests are MISSING - orphan recovery, gate context, JSON schema equivalence, exit code contract
5. ⚠️ **Tight coupling:** ddx agent execute-bead is NOT isolated from ddx work/try; they share orchestration logic, landing gates, and validation
6. ⚠️ **Unclear command hierarchy:** `ddx agent execute-loop` command not found in grep results; likely in agent_cmd.go but line refs suggest it may not exist yet

**For retirement to be safe:** Need to verify where `ddx agent execute-loop` lives, whether `ddx try` is meant as its replacement, and whether work/try are truly independent or still coupled through shared agent orchestration.

// TestDDxWork_ReadinessUnavailableOutput (AC1, AC6 / ddx-30bc30ed): when the
// intake service is unreachable, ddx work must print actionable
// readiness-unavailable output rather than exposing internal error prefixes.
// The test fails if that output is dropped.
func TestDDxWork_ReadinessUnavailableOutput(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		if strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake") {
			return nil, fmt.Errorf("readiness service unavailable: connection refused")
		}
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	out, _ := executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")

	// AC1: actionable readiness-unavailable line must appear in operator output.
	assert.Contains(t, out, "readiness check unavailable",
		"ddx work must print actionable readiness-unavailable output; got: %s", out)
	// AC6: doubled prefix must not appear in operator output.
	assert.NotContains(t, out, "pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake:",
		"pre-claim intake prefix must not be doubled in operator output")
}

// TestDDxWork_ReadinessTooLargeSkipsClaim (AC3, AC6 / ddx-30bc30ed): when
// intake classifies a bead as too_large, ddx work must not dispatch the
// implementer and must move the bead to operator-attention status.
func TestDDxWork_ReadinessTooLargeSkipsClaim(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		if strings.Contains(req.Prompt, "MODE: intake") {
			ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
			ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"needs_split\",\"rationale\":\"too large\",\"readiness_checks\":[{\"reason\":\"too_large\",\"verdict\":\"fail\",\"evidence\":\"AC spans three subsystems\"}]}"}`)}
			close(ch)
			return ch, nil
		}
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"ok"}`)}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := setupWorkIntakeFixture(t)
	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	_, _ = executeCommand(root, "work", "--once", "--no-review", "--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")

	// AC3: implementer must not be dispatched when intake classifies bead as too_large.
	stub.mu.Lock()
	executionSeen := stub.executionSeen
	stub.mu.Unlock()
	assert.False(t, executionSeen,
		"implementer must not be dispatched when intake classifies bead as too_large (needs_split)")

	// Bead must be moved to proposed (operator attention) after too_large classification.
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
	b, err := store.Get("ddx-intake-test")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusProposed, b.Status,
		"too_large bead must be parked to proposed status after intake rejection")
}

// TestExecuteBeadDoesNotCallResolveRoute (AC4 / ddx-6036e1bc): ddx agent
// execute-bead must not call ResolveRoute in the execution path. The stub
// installed by installExecuteCapturingStub returns an error from ResolveRoute,
// so any call to it would surface as a command failure containing
// "ResolveRoute called in execution path".
//
// Static guarantee: TestRoutinglintNonStatusFilesDoNotCallResolveRoute already
// ensures agent_execute_bead.go has no direct .ResolveRoute() call. This
// behavioral test adds a runtime check at the cmd layer.
func TestExecuteBeadDoesNotCallResolveRoute(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")

	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	_ = stub // stub.ResolveRoute returns error; any call would propagate up

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)

	// Seed a bead so execute-bead has something to load before reaching git.
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:    "ddx-no-resolve-route-test",
		Title: "execute-bead no ResolveRoute test",
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead",
		"ddx-no-resolve-route-test",
		"--harness", "agent",
	)
	// execute-bead may fail (no git repo), but must not call ResolveRoute.
	if err != nil {
		require.NotContains(t, err.Error(), "ResolveRoute called in execution path",
			"execute-bead must not call ResolveRoute (CONTRACT-003 / ddx-6036e1bc)")
	}
}

# agent-cmd-test-migration: Classification Audit

**Bead**: ddx-d8474e0e
**Execution**: 20260510T013243-b86bf611

## Deleted Test Files (cobra route depended on retired `ddx agent` surface)

| File | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `agent_execute_bead_test.go` | Tested `ddx agent execute-bead` cobra route; helpers extracted to `execute_bead_test_helpers_test.go` |
| `agent_execute_bead_e2e_test.go` | E2E tests for `ddx agent execute-bead`; retired with the command |
| `agent_execute_bead_gates_test.go` | Gate evaluation via `ddx agent execute-bead`; coverage migrated to `TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding` |
| `execute_bead_e2e_routing_test.go` | Routing E2E via `ddx agent execute-bead` route; retired |
| `agent_compare_config_test.go` | `ddx agent compare` cobra route tests |
| `agent_quorum_config_test.go` | `ddx agent quorum` cobra route tests |
| `agent_capabilities_test.go` | `ddx agent` capabilities tests |
| `agent_check_test.go` | `ddx agent check` cobra route tests |
| `agent_doctor_workers_test.go` | `ddx agent doctor/workers` cobra route tests |
| `agent_log_bead_test.go` | `ddx agent log` cobra route tests |
| `agent_metrics_test.go` | `ddx agent metrics` cobra route tests |
| `agent_models_test.go` | `ddx agent models` cobra route tests |
| `agent_providers_test.go` | `ddx agent providers` cobra route tests |
| `agent_route_status_test.go` | `ddx agent route-status` cobra route tests |
| `agent_run_config_test.go` | `ddx agent run` config tests |
| `agent_run_default_route_test.go` | `ddx agent run` default-route tests |
| `agent_run_dispatch_spec_test.go` | `ddx agent run` dispatch spec tests |
| `agent_run_overrides_e2e_test.go` | `ddx agent run` override E2E tests |
| `agent_run_profile_test.go` | `ddx agent run` profile tests |
| `agent_run_timeout_test.go` | `ddx agent run` timeout tests |
| `agent_usage_test.go` | `ddx agent usage` cobra route tests |
| `agent_workers_prune_test.go` | `ddx agent workers prune` cobra route tests |
| `agent_workers_stop_test.go` | `ddx agent workers stop` cobra route tests |
| `agent_executions_test.go` | `ddx agent executions` cobra route tests |
| `routinglint_test.go` | Routing lint tests tied to agent_cmd.go structure |
| `agent_metrics_review_evidence_test.go` | `computeReviewOutcomesReport` function, deleted with metrics agent command |

## Kept Test Files (engine logic, not cobra route)

| File | Status | Reason |
|------|--------|--------|
| `agent_execute_loop_costcap_test.go` | Kept | Tests engine cost-cap logic (now in work_impl.go) |
| `agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go` | Kept | Execute-loop E2E via `ddx work` cobra route |
| `agent_execute_loop_escalation_test.go` | Kept | Escalation logic via execute-loop engine |
| `agent_execute_loop_review_repair_test.go` | Kept | Review/repair logic via execute-loop engine |
| `agent_execute_loop_routing_reject_test.go` | Kept | Routing rejection logic via execute-loop engine |
| `agent_execute_loop_test.go` | Kept | Core execute-loop unit tests; uses fakeExecuteBeadGit/fakeAgentRunner |
| `agent_mode_guidance_test.go` | Kept | Mode guidance tests (no cobra route dependency) |
| `agent_output_test.go` | Kept | Output formatting tests (no cobra route dependency) |

## Modified Test Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `agent_run_passthrough_test.go` | Removed `TestExecuteBeadDoesNotCallResolveRoute` (used `ddx agent execute-bead`); kept all `ddx work`/`ddx try` tests; added `fmt` import for readiness tests from ddx-30bc30ed |
| `internal/agent/session_index_test.go` | `TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter`: updated entry `ddx agent run`→`ddx run` (path `cmd/agent_cmd.go`→`cmd/run.go`); removed "replay" entry (command retired with `ddx agent`) |

## New Test Helper Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `execute_bead_test_helpers_test.go` | `fakeExecuteBeadGit`, `fakeAgentRunner`, `newExecuteBeadFactory`, `fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit`; previously in `agent_execute_bead_test.go` |
| `run_test_helpers_test.go` | `stubAgentService`, `minimalProjectDir`, `executeCapturingStub`, `installExecuteCapturingStub`, `writeExecResult`; previously scattered across deleted files |

## New Required Tests (AC#10)

| Test | File | What it verifies |
|------|------|-----------------|
| `TestTry_RecoversOrphanedWorktreesBeforeSpawn` | `try_test.go` | `agent.RecoverOrphans` is called before executor build |
| `TestTry_AppliesGateContextToLanding` | `try_test.go` | `BuildLandingGateContext` is called when executor produces commits |
| `TestTry_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteBead` | `try_test.go` | Output contains bead:, status:, result_rev:, preserve_ref: fields |
| `TestTry_ExitCodeContract_0_1_2` | `try_test.go` | Status → exit code mapping: success=0, preserved=1, failed=2 |
| `TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop` | `work_test.go` | `ddx work --json --once` output contains attempts/successes/failures/no_ready_work/results |

## Prerequisite Lifts (AC#13 / ddx-ddacd4ff)

The following functions were lifted from `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go` into `cli/cmd/try.go`:

| Function | Previous location | New location |
|----------|------------------|--------------|
| `agent.RecoverOrphans` | `agent_execute_bead.go:168` | `try.go` (called before executor spawn) |
| `agent.BuildLandingGateContext` | `agent_execute_bead.go:206` | `try.go` (called in landing path when result has commits) |
| `agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService` | `agent_execute_bead.go:161` | N/A — `ddx try` operates per-bead (not loop), validation is service-side; documented as not applicable for per-bead invocation |

## Deleted Source Files (AC#1)

All `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` source files deleted:

| File | Subcommands |
|------|------------|
| `agent_cmd.go` | `ddx agent` root + `run`, `condense`, `list`, `capabilities`, `doctor`, `log`, `benchmark`, `replay`, `providers`, `models`, `check`, `route-status`, `metrics` |
| `agent_execute_bead.go` | `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try` |
| `agent_execute_loop_escalation.go` | Escalation logic (moved to work_impl.go) |
| `agent_execute_loop_review_repair.go` | Review/repair logic (moved to work_impl.go) |
| `agent_executions.go` | `ddx agent executions` |
| `agent_metrics.go` | `ddx agent metrics` |
| `agent_models.go` | `ddx agent models` |
| `agent_providers.go` | `ddx agent providers` |
| `agent_route_status.go` | `ddx agent route-status` |
| `agent_usage.go` | Renamed to `usage_stats.go` (no cobra route, utility functions) |
| `agent_workers.go` | `ddx agent workers` |
| `agent_check.go` | `ddx agent check` |
| `agent_doctor_workers.go` | `ddx agent doctor` worker health |

## Command Mapping Checklist (AC#3)

| Old command | New command | Status |
|-------------|------------|--------|
| `ddx agent run` | `ddx run` | Covered by `ddx run` (run.go) |
| `ddx agent execute-bead` | `ddx try` | Covered by `ddx try` (try.go) with engine lifts |
| `ddx agent execute-loop` | `ddx work` | Covered by `ddx work` (work.go + work_impl.go) |
| `ddx agent list` | Removed | No replacement needed; `ddx run --help` documents harnesses |
| `ddx agent doctor` | `ddx doctor` | Covered by existing `ddx doctor` command |
| `ddx agent log` | Removed | Logs available via `.ddx/executions/` directory |
| `ddx agent benchmark` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent replay` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent condense` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent capabilities` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent metrics` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent usage` | Retired cobra route; utility functions in `usage_stats.go` | |
| `ddx agent workers` | Removed | Worker management via server admin |
| `ddx agent check` | Removed | Retired with `ddx agent` surface |
| `ddx agent route-status` | Removed | Routing status via Fizeau layer |
| `ddx agent providers` | Removed | Provider enumeration via Fizeau layer |
| `ddx agent models` | Removed | Model enumeration via Fizeau layer |
| `ddx agent executions` | Removed | Executions browsable via `.ddx/executions/` |

All non-test `agent_*.go` source files are deleted. The remaining `agent_*_test.go` files are the kept engine-logic tests documented in the classification audit (AC#11). All acceptance criteria are satisfied:

- **AC#1**: All `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` source files deleted (0 non-test survivors)
- **AC#2**: `grep -rn 'ddx agent' cli/ docs/ .agents/skills/ .claude/skills/ skills/` → 0 hits
- **AC#3**: `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work` cover all prior operations (`run.go`, `try.go`, `work.go` present)
- **AC#7**: `go build ./...` green
- **AC#8**: `go test ./cmd/...` green (33.6s)
- **AC#9**: `lefthook run pre-commit` — all 14 hooks pass
- **AC#11**: Classification audit at `.ddx/executions/20260510T013243-b86bf611/agent-cmd-test-migration.md`

Two commits on HEAD: `87ccade27` (core retirement) and `8e56b2846` (AC#2 doc/skills cleanup).
